name: Feather Souffle Roll with Shrimp Filling
recipeIngredient: 2 packages Cream cheese, softened (3 oz. each)
recipeInstructions:
FEATHER SOUFFLE ROLL:
- Grease the bottom and sides of a 15x10x1-inch jellyroll pan with vegetable oil. Line the bottom of the pan with wax paper, allowing it to extend beyond the ends of the pan. Grease the wax paper, flour the wax paper, and grease the sides of the pan. Set aside.
- In a small saucepan, combine the flour and 1/2 cup milk, stirring until well blended. Add the remaining 1 cup milk. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until thickened.
- Beat the egg yolks in a small mixing bowl at high speed of an electric mixer until thick and pale. Gradually stir about one-fourth of the hot mixture into the yolks; add to the remaining hot mixture, stirring constantly. Stir in salt, pepper, nutmeg, and dissolved salt and garlic powder. Transfer the mixture to a large bowl and let cool.
- Beat the egg whites until stiff but not dry; fold into the milk mixture. Spread evenly in the prepared pan. Bake at 375°F for 20 to 25 minutes or until lightly browned.
- Remove from oven; loosen the edges of the souffle with a metal spatula. Cover the pan with a dampened cotton towel and invert the pan and towel together, turning the souffle out onto the towel. Carefully peel off the wax paper.
- Cover the souffle with a clean piece of wax paper, starting at the long side, roll up the souffle, wax paper, and towel together. Cool completely on a wire rack; chill 3 to 4 hours.
- Carefully unroll the souffle and spread with shrimp filling. Roll up tightly, wrap in plastic wrap, and chill for 3 hours. Remove the plastic wrap and cut into 27 (1/2-inch) slices using an electric knife. Garnish with whole shrimp and fresh parsley sprigs if desired. Yield: 9 appetizer servings.
SHRIMP FILLING:
- Bring water to a boil; add shrimp, and cook for 3 to 5 minutes or until the shrimp turn pink. Drain well; rinse with cold water. Cover and chill.
- Peel, devein, and chop the shrimp; set aside.
- Combine cream cheese and next 5 ingredients in a medium mixing bowl; beat at medium speed of an electric mixer until smooth. Stir in chopped shrimp and green onions. Yield: about 1 1/2 cups.
